title: UNION and INTERSECT
content:
### UNION and INTERSECT

Set operators combine results from multiple SELECT queries into a single result set.

---

### UNION

UNION combines results from two or more SELECT statements, removing duplicate rows:

```sql
SELECT name, email FROM customers
UNION
SELECT name, email FROM employees;
```

### UNION ALL

UNION ALL combines results without removing duplicates. It is faster than UNION:

```sql
SELECT city FROM shipping_addresses
UNION ALL
SELECT city FROM billing_addresses;
-- May include duplicate cities
```

| Operator | Duplicates | Performance |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| UNION | Removed | Slower (needs sorting) |
| UNION ALL | Kept | Faster |

### INTERSECT

INTERSECT returns only rows that appear in ALL result sets:

```sql
-- Names that appear in both tables
SELECT name FROM employees
INTERSECT
SELECT name FROM customers;
```

### EXCEPT / MINUS

EXCEPT returns rows from the first query that do NOT appear in the second:

```sql
-- Customers who have never placed an order
SELECT id FROM customers
EXCEPT
SELECT customer_id FROM orders;
```

### Rules for Set Operations

| Rule | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Same number of columns | All SELECT statements must have the same column count |
| Compatible data types | Corresponding columns must have compatible data types |
| ORDER BY at end | Only one ORDER BY at the very end of the combined query |
| Column names | Result uses column names from the first SELECT |

```sql
SELECT name, salary FROM employees
WHERE department = 'Engineering'
UNION
SELECT name, salary FROM contractors
WHERE department = 'Engineering'
ORDER BY name;
```

> **Key Point:** Each SELECT in a set operation must have the same number of columns with compatible data types. ORDER BY applies to the entire combined result.
codeExample:
SELECT product_name AS item, 'Product' AS type FROM products
UNION ALL
SELECT service_name, 'Service' FROM services
ORDER BY item;

quiz.question: What is the difference between UNION and UNION ALL?
options: UNION removes duplicates, UNION ALL keeps them
UNION ALL removes duplicates, UNION keeps them
UNION works with more data types

quiz.question: How many ORDER BY clauses can a UNION query have?
options: One at the end of the combined query
